title: Drug Database UI: authoritative dataset alignment, header text, and academic neutral theme

requirements.summary: Ensure the Drug Database UI reflects the expanded authoritative dataset (550+) and stays in sync after Refresh & Verify.
acceptanceCriteria: The dataset used to seed the backend contains more than 550 total approved+banned entries spanning multiple categories (e.g., antibiotics, painkillers, FDCs, vitamins, other).
The Drug Database UI counts (total/approved/banned) match the backend authoritative dataset after seeding and after Refresh & Verify.

file_operations.description: Tighten React Query configuration for authoritative drug queries (useGetAllDrugs/useGetApprovedDrugs/useGetBannedDrugs/useGetCategorizedDrugs) to reliably refetch on mount and avoid stale counts after refresh; ensure Refresh & Verify mutation continues to invalidate all relevant drug query keys used by the Drug Database UI.

Add a small, prominent authoritative-dataset status line in the Drug Database header area that surfaces backend-derived totals (total/approved/banned) and highlights when totals are unexpectedly low (<550) so mismatches are visible immediately after seeding and after Refresh & Verify.

requirements.summary: Update the Drug Database module header to the required academic title string.
acceptanceCriteria: The specified header text is visible at the top of the Drug Database module on both desktop and mobile widths.

file_operations.description: Replace the current Drug Database module title with the exact required header text “Complete CDSCO Drug Database | 500+ Drugs | Auto-Updated” and ensure it is presented prominently and responsively within the module header.

requirements.summary: Apply a consistent professional academic neutral theme across the Drug Database module (cards, tables, badges, buttons, banners) without introducing blue/purple as dominant primary styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Drug Database module styling appears consistent across tabs (All/Approved/Banned) and in dark/light themes.
No new primary brand reliance on blue/purple gradients is introduced as the dominant style in this module (existing elements may be adjusted to match the new theme).

file_operations.description: Refactor module-level styling to a neutral academic palette (stone/emerald/amber/red) by updating Card, header, table header, badges (status/category/source), buttons, and banners to remove dominant blue/purple gradients and ensure consistent typography hierarchy across tabs.

Align the Ban Trends dialog styling with the same neutral academic theme (backgrounds, borders, headings, and summary cards) so it visually matches the Drug Database module in both light/dark modes without relying on blue/purple as the primary accent.

If needed to support consistent module styling, adjust or add minimal theme tokens/utility conventions (using existing Tailwind + CSS variable approach) to reinforce neutral academic colors and typography hierarchy without introducing new global stylesheet entry points.
